div1.entryFree:
Thrill, 1) to affect with a sharp, shivering sensation running through the system: to reside in --ing region of thick-ribbed ice, Meas. III, 1, 123. --ed with remorse, Lr. IV, 2, 73.
2) to have a shivering sensation running through the system, to be chilled: to t. and shake even at the crying of your nation's crow, John V, 2, 143. doth not thy blood t. at it? H4A II, 4, 407. I have a faint cold fear --s through my veins, Rom. IV, 3, 15.
